a:r.a:t: KPMG’s Pre-Deal Purchase Price Allocation (“PPA”) benchmarking analysis provides an indication of key outcomes associated with the revaluation of intangible assets as part of a Pre-Deal PPA exercise and is based on data extracted from benchmark PPAs operating in a similar industry as the Target.

While this analysis provides an indication of key outcomes, benchmarking analysis on its own is inherently limited and the outcome should be treated as such. Therefore, the benchmarking analysis may not necessarily be suitable for financial reporting or tax purposes. If needed, a more detailed PPA analysis can be performed by KPMG Valuation Services professionals.
Our KPMG database includes a total of [
] selected transactions based on public and proprietary data available for the Target’s industry [
]. The industry group is based on Capital IQ’s industry classification coding.
Based on the above, the KPMG Pre-Deal PPA benchmarking tool provides the following parameters:
Fair value as a percentage of entity value by intangible asset category
: indication of intangible assets recognized as a range of entity value.
Percentage of deals with recognized intangible asset category
: provides a snapshot of the typically recognized intangible assets in the Target’s industry.
Royalty rates and economic useful lives ranges
: information of ranges of royalty rates commonly applied for intangibles in the Target industry and useful lives estimation.
Amortization impact pattern of recognized intangible assets
: projected earnings impact of amortization of intangible assets.
industry applicable to the Target is selected following the Capital IQ industry classification.
Based on our KPMG proprietary database, an indication of observed categories of intangible assets as well as certain parameters are provided.
Note: Be mindful that the fair value of fixed assets and other assets and liabilities are not in scope of the benchmarking tool.

p:cNvPr.name: Rectangle: Top Corners One Rounded and One Snipped 14

a:r.a:t: Please coordinate with your local Valuation team and consult the
before applying Valuation+ to the report.
